label: Computer Networks
description: The subject area covered by the course.

label: Classification of Computer Networks
description: The categorization method for computer networks discussed in the session.

label: Local Area Network (LAN)
description: A computer network that interconnects computers within a limited area.

label: Metropolitan Area Network (MAN)
description: A computer network that interconnects users or computers in a geographic region of the size of a metropolitan area (City).

label: Wide Area Network (WAN)
description: A telecommunications network that extends over a large geographical area for the primary purpose of computer networking.

label: Wired LAN
description: A type of Local Area Network that uses cables.

label: Wireless LAN
description: A type of Local Area Network that is wireless.

label: Ethernet
description: The well known and most popular technology in wired LAN, used to connect two or more nodes.

label: Ethernet Straight Through Cable
description: A cable used to connect devices of different kinds.

label: Ethernet Crossover Cable
description: A cable used to connect a computer with another computer directly.

label: Hub
description: An intermediary device in a wired local area network.

label: Switch
description: An intermediary device in a wired local area network.

label: Wi-Fi
description: An example technology for a wireless LAN.

label: Router
description: A device needed to connect two local area networks.

label: Bridge
description: A device needed to connect two local area networks.

label: Telecommunications Network
description: Any communications at a distance.

label: Internet
description: A wide wide area network where many local area networks, metropolitan area networks, and wide area networks across the world are connected to each other.

label: New Trends in Computer Networks
description: Emerging trends in the computer networking field.

label: Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)
description: A scenario where employees are encouraged by their employers to bring their own device and connect to the office network.

label: Online Collaboration
description: The ability for participants to collaborate with others from wherever they are with the help of computer networks.

label: Cloud Computing
description: The on-demand availability of computer resources, especially data storage and computing power, without direct active management by the user.

label: Google Drive
description: An example of cloud computing where one can store files and folders.
